purpose: Satellite Analyzer processes multispectral satellite imagery to identify land-use changes and surface anomalies. It combines image preprocessing, feature extraction, and report generation so analysts can review geographic risk signals quickly.
one_liner: Detects satellite imagery changes and summarizes geographic risk signals quickly.

modules.name: ingestion
modules.description: Downloads imagery metadata and normalizes source scenes for analysis.

modules.name: change_detection
modules.description: Compares image tiles across time windows to identify meaningful changes.

incomplete_features: Cloud masking is planned but currently uses a placeholder threshold.

module_citations.name: compare_tiles
module_citations.source:
def compare_tiles(before, after):
    return diff_score(before, after)

incomplete_citations.name: mask_clouds

setup_instructions.setup_markdown:
## Prerequisites

- Python 3.12+
- GDAL-compatible raster tooling

## Installation

Install dependencies with `pip install -r requirements.txt` and run `python -m satellite_analyzer`.

folders.description: Core package for imagery ingestion, analysis, and reporting.

root_files.description: Project overview and local setup instructions.
